scenario_name: Scenario 21 — Multi-Parcel Tracking Partial Delivery Detection
justification: All pass criteria met: Shipment vs Parcel clearly distinguished as aggregate root and child entity with independent lifecycles. Shipment status explicitly described as derived/projected from parcel states in multiple places (ubiquitous language 'Derived Status', BR-2 with testable assertion 'no public API that directly sets shipment status'). At least 4 parcel-level states defined (Created, InTransit, OutForDelivery, Delivered, Lost, Returned, Disputed, Held, Found). Business invariants cover partial delivery detection (BR-3, BR-4), lost parcel escalation (BR-5), and parcel-level dispute/refund granularity (BR-7). Nine non-trivial edge cases surfaced including race conditions, lost-then-found parcels, customs holds, and zero-parcel shipments. Ten detailed acceptance criteria provided in Given/When/Then format.

observations.text: Derivation rule is the centerpiece of the analysis — BR-2 explicitly states shipment status must never be set independently, with a testable assertion that no public API exists to do so
Exceptionally thorough state machine with 9+ parcel states including non-happy-path states (Lost, Disputed, Returned, Held, Found)
Edge cases go well beyond minimum — includes lost-then-found with compensating events, race conditions with optimistic locking, customs holds, and zero-parcel rejection
Disputes explicitly require parcel-level tracking numbers (BR-7) with validation error on shipment-only disputes
10 acceptance criteria in structured Given/When/Then format covering happy path, edge cases, and migration
Business impact analysis with stakeholder breakdown adds significant value beyond the rubric requirements
BR-9 (post-dispatch composition freeze) and BR-10 (single-parcel consistency) show deep domain thinking beyond the obvious requirements

Could have been more explicit about the shipment status derivation formula — the mapping rules (all delivered → Fully Delivered, mix → Partially Delivered, etc.) are spread across BR-2/BR-3/BR-4 rather than consolidated into one clear derivation table
Data migration acceptance criterion (AC-8) demonstrates awareness of practical operational concerns

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Refactoring a logistics platform from shipment-level tracking (one tracking number, one status per shipment) to parcel-level tracking (each parcel has its own tracking number and lifecycle). This enables partial delivery detection — knowing that 3 of 5 parcels arrived while 2 are still in transit or lost. Currently the system treats a shipment as a single atomic unit, which makes it impossible to represent partial fulfillment, per-parcel exceptions, or gra
